summary: This role poses a significant threat to your physical wellbeing and career longevity, as 80-90% of practitioners suffer from work-related musculoskeletal disorders that often lead to early retirement. Furthermore, your aversion to complex math and fast-paced analytical reasoning directly conflicts with the job's requirement for calculating advanced hemodynamics and making high-stakes diagnostic judgments under pressure. The mandatory on-call rotations and extreme emotional labor required in hospital settings also fail to meet your criteria for predictable hours and a sustainable, low-stress career.
